title: Body Systems
description: Explores what it takes to be an Olympic athlete and how athletes use their muscles, endurance, and body systems for different sports.

title: Local and Large-scale Farming
description: Investigates where food comes from and develops food literacy by examining how foods are grown and produced before reaching stores and markets.

title: Safety First!
description: Examines safety equipment in sport and why protective gear differs from one sport to another to keep athletes safe.

title: Bionic Design
description: Features emerging technologies, STEM contributions, and Canadian innovations such as robotic arms that are making a difference.

title: Changes in Body Systems
description: Looks at how the human body is made up of many cells and systems that work together to help it function.

title: Global Positioning Systems
description: Introduces how technology such as the global positioning system (GPS) works and the role it plays in everyday life.

title: Understanding Body Systems
description: Focuses on the major human body systems, including the nervous system, and how each sends signals or supports the body.

title: Comparative Anatomy
description: Explores what anatomy is by comparing the similarities and differences between the structures and systems of humans and various animals.

title: Food Labels
description: Examines nutrition facts tables and ingredient lists required on prepackaged foods in Canada, including serving size, calories, and key nutrients.

title: Code It! The Digestive System
description: Uses coding and sequencing concepts to learn about the organs and processes of the human digestive system and how to care for the body.

title: Transportation and Energy Consumption
description: Looks at different types of transportation and the sources of energy used to power them, such as electric cars.

title: Transportation and Body Systems
description: Explores active, human-powered transportation methods like walking and biking and how they connect to health and physical activity.

title: The Evolution of Bicycles
description: Traces how the bicycle has evolved since the late 1400s by comparing the similarities and differences of bicycle designs over time.

title: The Benefits of Cycling
description: Examines cycle-friendly communities around the world and how those spaces are designed to support cycling.

title: Transportation Methods
description: Explores transportation methods established before modern vehicles, including Indigenous perspectives from the Kitchenuhmaykoosib Inninuwug (K.I.) First Nation.

title: Transportation for a Greener Future
description: Investigates how different types of transportation move and compares their similarities and differences with a focus on greener options.

title: Code It! If... Then...
description: Uses conditional (if/then) coding concepts while exploring active transportation types that can be used based on where someone lives.

title: Winter Wear
description: Explores activities and technology used in extreme winter weather, such as off-road tracked vehicles, and the clothing needed for cold conditions.

title: City Planning, Part 1
description: Examines images of city spaces and how those constructed spaces are planned and designed.

title: City Planning, Part 2
description: Continues city planning with a focus on accessibility and sustainability to make environments usable for everyone and protect the planet for future generations.

title: Manufacturing and Our Changing World
description: Introduces manufacturing by exploring how everyday products like packaged food, clothing, and technology are made.

title: Producing Plastics
description: Examines how many everyday products are made of plastic and how plastic products are manufactured around the world.

title: Pulp and Paper
description: Explores common paper products and how paper is manufactured, including its effects on people, animals, and the environment.

title: What is Waste?
description: Looks at how waste is sorted into categories such as organics, garbage, mixed containers, and paper, and why separate bins matter.

title: Indigenous-led Conservation
description: Shares perspectives from the Kitchenuhmaykoosib Inninuwug (K.I.) First Nation on their relationship to land and water and how it helps protect natural resources.

title: Forms of Energy
description: Explains that energy is the ability to do work and exists in many forms, including potential and kinetic energy from sources like the sun, wind, and fuel.

title: Code It! We're Fired Up!
description: Uses coding concepts to explore what fire is and how it is created when heat forces gases from a burning object to combine with oxygen at the ignition point.

title: The Impact of Technology
description: Examines how technology, from steam engines to modern devices, helps people and what these innovations have in common.

title: Consuming Energy Responsibly
description: Explores how people consume energy daily and how it can move and change but cannot be destroyed, encouraging responsible energy use.

title: Sustainable Manufacturing
description: Looks at fashion as a global process and the series of steps used to manufacture clothing, with attention to sustainability.

title: The Roots of STEM
description: Introduces what STEM learning is and what the acronym (science, technology, engineering, and math) stands for through classroom examples.

title: Matter Matters
description: Explores the states of matter through videos and examples, describing what students notice about each state.

title: Sustainable Design
description: Examines how architects use science and technology to design homes, buildings, and other structures that are environmentally friendly.

title: Renewable and Non renewable Energy Sources
description: Looks at how Ontario generates electricity from diverse energy sources and how electrical energy can be transformed into other forms of energy.

title: States of Matter Changes in Everyday Life
description: Explores the states of matter—solid, liquid, and gas—and how they appear in everyday situations.

title: Natural Disasters
description: Examines weather and natural events such as storms, snowstorms, and smog through images and descriptions of conditions across Canada.

title: The Power of Water
description: Uses a poem about natural wonders and a video on waterfalls to explore the power and movement of water.

title: Animals Adaptations
description: Explores how animals and insects adapt to their environments, including camouflage as a type of adaptation.

title: Stable Structures
description: Examines what makes structures strong and stable using examples like skyscrapers and key design elements.

title: Code It! Make Change
description: Uses coding concepts to explore how matter changes, including physical and chemical changes that occur naturally or are caused by humans.
